title: Abdominal bloating

examination.text: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.
examination.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed abdominal, systemic, inflammatory, and cancer-concern documentation without asserting that examination occurred or that concern is absent.
examination.evidence_extract: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.
investigations.rationale: The exact adult IBS symptom-profile section supports documenting bloating, distension, meal association, bowel-pattern change, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, mucus, duration, and impact without assigning IBS or another cause.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.
investigations.rationale: The exact diagnostic-test section supports recording already reviewed blood and coeliac-test context only when the adult IBS criteria and clinician context apply; it does not order tests.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.
investigations.rationale: The exact follow-up section supports clinician-entered follow-up, red-flag reassessment, and patient discussion without generating dietary, medicine, investigation, or referral advice.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

source_limitations: Acute onset, pregnancy, pelvic symptoms, visible distension, examination, ovarian context, obstruction, ascites, medicine, diet, exact tests, diagnosis, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
